malay: Coklat Tanda Tangan Oat Susu Ais

ontology.foodex2_gap_note: No exact FoodEx2 code exists for iced oatmilk chocolate beverage; A0CKN (chocolate-based beverages) is the closest defensible category-level anchor in the FoodEx2 hierarchy.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving of iced oatmilk signature chocolate is approximately 250 grams, equivalent to a medium-sized cup.

nutrient_source.title: Iced Oatmilk Signature Chocolate
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient data directly from HPB Singapore for this specific product.

gi_source.title: Oat milk (sweetened) and chocolate syrup — analogous estimate
gi_source.source_match_notes: GI estimated at 60 (medium) based on sweetened oat milk entries and chocolate syrup sugar load in the University of Sydney GI Database. No exact GI entry exists for this specific branded beverage. GL per serving calculated as (60 × 10.36 × 2.5) / 100 = 15.5, rounded to 15.0.

hypertension.key_factor: High sugar content from added syrups independently raises blood pressure risk
hypertension.guidance: This beverage contains 9.94g sugar/100g from added vanilla and mocha syrups, which independently elevates blood pressure risk per AHA 2018 guidance; individuals with hypertension should avoid sugar-sweetened beverages and request unsweetened alternatives.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content and zero dietary fibre cause rapid blood glucose elevation
type2_diabetes.guidance: With 9.94g sugar/100g and 0g dietary fibre, this sugar-sweetened beverage triggers rapid postprandial blood glucose spikes and is unsuitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes; unsweetened oat milk with unsweetened cocoa is a safer alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High added sugar intake contributing to systemic inflammation and dyslipidemia
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Regular consumption of high-sugar beverages such as this one is associated with elevated triglycerides and systemic inflammation, increasing cardiovascular disease risk; individuals should limit intake and choose unsweetened beverage options.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Iced Oatmilk Signature Chocolate is a chilled café-style beverage made with oat milk as the dairy-free base, flavored with vanilla syrup and mocha-flavored syrup, and served over ice. The drink delivers a creamy, mildly sweet chocolate flavor with a subtle coffee undertone from the mocha syrup. Oat milk provides a naturally smooth texture and is a popular plant-based alternative to dairy milk. However, the significant addition of flavored syrups results in a high sugar content of 9.94g per 100g and negligible dietary fibre, making this a sugar-sweetened beverage by nutritional classification. Per 100g, it provides 42 kcal, 0g protein, 0.25g fat, and 10.36g carbohydrates. Due to its high sugar load and absence of fibre, it is not recommended for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. It is suitable for vegans and is dairy-free, though it contains gluten from oats. This beverage is commonly found in Western-style cafes in Singapore and is consumed as a snack or treat.
common_user_queries: Sugar content in oat milk chocolate drink

confidence_reason: Nutrient data sourced directly from HPB Singapore for this specific product with confidence 0.95. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database based on sweetened oat milk and chocolate syrup entries.
notes: Protein_g is confirmed zero per HPB Singapore data; this is a syrup-and-oat-milk beverage with no protein-contributing ingredients at meaningful levels. Dietary_fibre_g is confirmed zero per HPB data despite oat milk base, indicating the syrup-dominant formulation contributes negligible fibre. Saturated_fat_g and magnesium_mg are confirmed zero per HPB data. Monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid profile, vitamin panel, and moisture data for this specific beverage are not reported in the HPB Singapore source. FoodEx2 code A0CKN used as closest defensible category-level anchor for chocolate-based beverages; no exact FoodEx2 code exists for iced oatmilk chocolate beverages.

q1_food_identity.notes: Canonical English name present; multilingual identity satisfied for Singaporean cuisine (Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, Hindi).

q2_core_macro_completeness_and_source_in.notes: All four core macros (energy_kcal, protein_g, fat_g, carbohydrate_g) present and sourced from HPB Singapore with confidence 0.95.

q3_secondary_nutrient_completeness.notes: dietary_fibre_g, sodium_mg, saturated_fat_g, and sugar_g are all present as numeric values and supported by HPB source.

q4_glycemic_completeness.notes: GI (60), gi_category (medium), glycemic_load_per_serving (15.0), and gi_source all present. GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database, documented in source_match_notes.

q5_ontological_alignment.notes: FoodOn URI (FOODON_00002446 — chocolate drink) is a defensible exact match. FoodEx2 code A0CKN (chocolate-based beverages) is a valid category-level code with analogous match type and gap note documented. SNOMED CT 226210007 (chocolate drink) is a defensible exact match. No codes are invented.

q6_llm_ready_semantics.notes: natural_language_description exceeds 200 characters and covers preparation, ingredients, taste, texture, nutritional profile, clinical relevance, and cultural context.

q7_health_context.notes: All three required conditions present: Hypertension (high concern), Type 2 Diabetes (high concern), Cardiovascular Disease (moderate concern). Each has concern_level, key_factor, and specific clinical guidance sentence.

q8_aliases_and_multilingual_synonym_cove.notes: All aliases_by_language string values appear verbatim in the flat aliases array. Coverage includes English variants, Chinese simplified and traditional, Malay, Tamil, and Hindi.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 3: Replaced invalid FoodEx2 code 'A0012#F00000005' with valid category-level code A0CKN (chocolate-based beverages) and set foodex2_match_type to 'analogous' with a documented gap note explaining the substitution.
Expanded gi_source source_match_notes to include GL calculation basis.
Expanded notes field to explicitly document all -1 fields and zero-macro justifications per g3 and g4 requirements.
Verified all aliases_by_language string values appear verbatim in flat aliases array (q8 consistency check passed).
Preserved all correct sections from iteration 2 draft.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: Record passes all six hard gates and scores 8/8. The critical fix in iteration 3 was replacing the invalid FoodEx2 code format with a valid category-level code A0CKN, resolving the g5_ontology_honesty gate failure from iteration 2. All multilingual naming, nutrient data, GI, health context, and LLM semantics sections are complete and well-justified.
